scope_note: QA Fibonacci-Orbit Index Correspondence cert [282]. Primary source: Wall, D. D. (1960). Fibonacci series modulo m. American Mathematical Monthly, 67(6), 525-532. DOI: 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541. Mechanism: cert [281] (Pisano-Orbit Correspondence) + cert [279] (Orbit Access Theorem). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): for n >= 1, the mod-9 orbit class of the Fibonacci number F_n (used as an a-value in the route enumeration b+2e=a) is determined solely by n mod 12: mul_9 iff 12|n (i.e. 9|F_n); mul_3_not_9 iff n == 4 or 8 mod 12 (i.e. 3|F_n but 9 not divides F_n); coprime_to_3 otherwise (3 not divides F_n). Wall (1960) proves: rank of apparition alpha(3)=4 (so 3|F_n iff 4|n) and alpha(9)=12 (so 9|F_n iff 12|n). Corollary of cert [281]: the Pisano period of 9 is 24, so F_n mod 9 repeats with period 24, and within each period mul_9 appears at positions == 0 mod 12, mul_3_not_9 at == 4 or 8 mod 12. Verified exhaustively for n=1..48 (two full Pisano periods). Cert does NOT claim the orbit class determines the orbit PERIOD of F_n (that is cert [281]). Cert does NOT assert the correspondence extends to Fibonacci numbers mod m for arbitrary m. Theorem NT compliance: F_n is computed by integer recurrence; mod 9 divisibility check is pure integer arithmetic; no float feedback into QA layer. Lineage: discovered 2026-05-30 as direct corollary of cert [281] and Wall (1960) rank-of-apparition theory.
